top_signals.title: OpenAI Leans Toward Pushing IPO to 2027, NYT Says
top_signals.summary: OpenAI is leaning toward holding off on an initial public offering until 2027, the New York Times reported, citing three people involved in the company’s deliberations.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: What Happened to Big Oil's Green Pivot?
top_signals.summary: Over the past 20 years, the story seemed straightforward. Big Oil would gradually become Big Energy. Over time, oil majors would use their balance sheets, engineering expertise, and global project-management skills to build wind farms, solar projects, hydrogen hubs, carbon capture networks, and renewable power businesses. Big Oil did make major investments in renewables. And that transition is still happening in parts of the energy sector. But among the oil majors, the strategy has become far more selective. The latest example is Equinor. The Norwegian…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Bitcoin ETF Flows Explained: What ETF Inflows and Outflows Mean for Investors
top_signals.summary: Bitcoin ETF flows refer to the movement of money into and out of bitcoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs). When investors buy ETF shares, new money enters the fund and creates an inflow. When investors sell shares and capital leaves the fund, an outflow occurs. Because most U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs hold actual bitcoin, these flows are […]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: The 2026 War on the Rocks Summer Fiction Reading List
top_signals.summary:
You can tell a lot about a person from what fiction they keep on their nightstand. Once a year, we make the staff, contributing editors, and show hosts of War on the Rocks tell on themselves.Kerry AndersonThe Midnight Library, Matt Haig (2020). Drawing on evergreen themes from books and movies such as Sliding Doors or It’s a Wonderful Life, The Midnight Library puts a fresh spin on questions like what’s the meaning of life, how does our presence (or absence) in the world affect those around us, what would have happened if we had made different choices, and are there
